changes.reason: Current cap at 4.5 excludes the -513.4 charm_pin_pullback_long tail with pullback-from-high 9.25; moving to 10 reopens the previously successful profile-matched tail-risk envelope without touching global entry quality.

The worst charm-pin tail had rolling-high distance 14.25; current cap 6 misses this high-extension/recent-high rejection state. The 15 boundary was historically accepted and is narrow enough to avoid generic extended-VWAP pruning.

The worst tail setup quality was 61.77, just below the current 70 floor. Lowering to 60 captures the high-extension charm-pin tail box while avoiding a broad setup-quality filter that would starve profitable trades.

The worst charm-pin tail had negative VWAP acceleration at -0.0179, while protected extended-VWAP winners have median acceleration near flat. Setting the max to 0.02 keeps the box focused on stalled or decelerating extension rather than fast continuation.

expected_effect: Should reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst/average MAE by catching the current -513.4 high-extension delayed-progress tail while preserving pullback_long, 1DTE, squeeze, and charm-wall convexity. Main tradeoff is losing a few profitable but slow charm-pin recoveries; reject if total net profit falls below 98% of run_029 or protected fast extended-VWAP winners drop materially.
label: charm-pin-tail-box-repair
rationale: Use one runtime-risk/surgical charm-pin family, not another take-profit or broad entry-pruning run. The active tail-risk signature is profile-matched but currently misses the worst charm_pin_pullback_long row because its pullback, rolling-high, and setup bounds are too tight. Prior memory shows this same tail-box direction improved objective and net profit, while the latest broad extended-VWAP and RSI-slope probes either retained too little PnL or left worst MAE unchanged.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long contributes $1261.5 across 34 VALID_0DTE trades with fast median hold near 5 minutes, but owns the global -513.4 MAE. The worst row combines ES-VWAP 26.69, pullback-from-high 9.25, rolling-high distance 14.25, negative VWAP acceleration, RSI 42.42, ADX 17.95, and delayed TP after 80 minutes.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Tail Box
strategy_hypotheses.risk: The tail box can still remove profitable delayed recoveries because the bad trade ultimately closed green; require strict PnL and protected-winner retention before promotion.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high ES extension, recent-high rejection/pullback, weak momentum warnings, and stalled VWAP acceleration.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and slow_12h remains 0.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: Analyzer path stats show the worst tail had MFE after MAE and first +1.5 points only after 80 minutes, while protected fast extended-VWAP winners usually resolve quickly. Static config can only approximate this behavior.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Path-aware logic can overfit a small tail sample unless validated against protected fast-winner UTCS and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension entries that fail to make early positive progress after entry.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE below the current -513.4 while retaining >=90% profile PnL, >=95% protected fast-winner count, and total score flat or better.

strategy_plan_reference.market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, but hidden MAE is material: average MAE about -98....
